name: Original Tremolo
description: The original double-locking tremolo; hardened steel base plate and CNC-machined steel saddles with a 37mm nickel-plated brass sustain block

name: 1000 Series Tremolo
description: Korean-made Original-spec tremolo with hardened steel saddles and 37mm nickel-plated brass block; sold direct-to-consumer

name: Special Tremolo
description: Budget double-locking tremolo matching the Original design but using zinc alloy saddles and a zinc alloy sustain block

name: 1500 Series Tremolo
description: Enhanced 1000 Series with stainless steel screws and push-in tremolo arm; available to guitar manufacturers only

name: Pro Tremolo
description: Low-profile double-locking tremolo with narrower .400 inch string spacing; requires a dedicated cavity route

name: FRX Tremolo
description: Surface-mount double-locking tremolo for Tune-o-matic guitars; swaps the existing bridge and stopbar with no body routing required

name: Rail Tail Bridge
description: Full-contact hardtail bridge that retrofits a Strat-style 6-point tremolo route; available in narrow (2-1/16 inch) and wide (2-7/32 inch) variants

name: Original Hot Rod Tremolo
description: Original-dimension double-locking tremolo upgraded with a fat brass sustain block and stainless steel screws for enhanced sustain

name: 7-String Original Tremolo
description: Original-spec double-locking tremolo scaled for seven strings; hardened steel construction with 37mm nickel-plated brass block

name: 8-String Tremolo
description: Double-locking tremolo for eight-string guitars; 53.8mm nut width and 112.75mm bridge width to cover the extended string spread

name: Titanium Tremolo
description: Full-titanium double-locking tremolo developed with TiSonix; tested to 12000+ cycles with no measurable knife-edge wear

name: FRX Titanium Tremolo
description: Limited-edition US-made all-titanium surface-mount FRX tremolo; individually numbered for traceability
